assertion_results.text: The answer recommends writing a failing test first and clearly references the TDD cycle or red-green-refactor order
assertion_results.evidence: Explicit: “Follow a tight red–green–refactor TDD loop.” Steps include “Create the test first,” “Run the test to see it fail (Red),” “Make the test pass … (Green),” and “Refactor safely (Refactor).”

assertion_results.text: The answer says to write only the minimal implementation needed to make the test pass before refactoring
assertion_results.evidence: Step 4: “Make the test pass with the simplest code (Green) – Implement the minimal logic to satisfy only that test. – Avoid pre-optimizing or generalizing.”

assertion_results.text: The answer stays focused on workflow order instead of expanding into general tooling setup
assertion_results.evidence: It goes beyond workflow with tooling/details: “Use pytest.mark.parametrize,” “Add Hypothesis/property-based tests,” “If using mypy/ruff, run them now,” and “Run the full suite and coverage.” Also includes many extra steps (properties, quality gates) beyond the core order.
